api/spec/packages/aip/lib/rules.purpose: Custom TypeSpec linter rules enforcing AIP naming conventions and structural constraints for the v3 API spec. These rules run during `make gen-api` (tsp compile) and fail the build on errors or emit warnings on violations.

patterns.name: createRule factory
patterns.description: Every rule is defined using `createRule` from `@typespec/compiler`, returning an object of TypeSpec node visitor callbacks (model, modelProperty, enum, operation, interface, union). Never use ad-hoc functions.
patterns.example: export const myRule = createRule({ name: '...', severity: 'warning'|'error', messages: {...}, create: (context) => ({ model: (node) => { ... } }) })

patterns.name: severity: error vs warning
patterns.description: Rules that must block code generation use `severity: 'error'` (casingErrorsRule, friendlyNameRule, operationIdKebabCaseRule). Style guidance uses `severity: 'warning'`. New rules must pick severity intentionally.
patterns.example: casingErrorsRule: enum member values must be snake_case — error. casingRule: model names must be PascalCase — warning.

patterns.name: casing contracts
patterns.description: Model names: PascalCase (isPascalCaseNoAcronyms). Non-path model properties: snake_case (isSnakeCase). Path parameters: camelCase (isCamelCaseNoAcronyms). Enum names/members: PascalCase. Enum member values: snake_case. @operationId values: kebab-case. All checked via utils.js helpers.
patterns.example: path param `subscriptionId` → camelCase OK. body field `plan_id` → snake_case OK. operationId `list-meters` → kebab-case OK.

patterns.name: @friendlyName required on all named types
patterns.description: All named models, enums, unions, and non-Endpoints/Operations interfaces must have `@friendlyName`. Interfaces ending in `Endpoints` or `Operations` must NOT have `@friendlyName`. This is an error-severity rule.

patterns.name: @summary required on operations
patterns.description: Every operation must have a `@summary` decorator. Missing summary is a warning that surfaces in linting output.

patterns.name: @doc required on models, enums, unions, and properties
patterns.description: All named models, enums, unions, and their non-special properties (excluding `_` and `contentType`) require a `@doc` decorator. Models ending in `Response` are exempt from property-level doc checks.
patterns.example: @doc("A usage meter") model Meter { @doc("Unique slug") slug: string; }

patterns.name: Composition over inheritance
patterns.description: Model inheritance via `extends` is flagged as a warning if the base model lacks `@discriminator`. Prefer spread (`...BaseModel`) or `model is BaseModel` for composition.
patterns.example: model Foo { ...BaseFields; }  // OK. model Foo extends Base { }  // warning unless @discriminator on Base.

key_file_guides.role: Exports isPascalCaseNoAcronyms, isCamelCaseNoAcronyms, isSnakeCase, isKebabCase — shared by all rule files. PascalCase exceptions: OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new casing utility must be added here and imported; do not inline regex in rule files.

key_file_guides.role: Enforces naming casing on models, properties, enums, enum members. Two rules: `casing` (warning) for structural names; `casing-aip-errors` (error) for enum member values.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Path parameters (`$path` decorator) use camelCase, not snake_case — the rule explicitly checks for `$path` decorator presence before switching casing check.

key_file_guides.role: Error-severity rule requiring @friendlyName on all named models, enums, unions, and interfaces (except *Endpoints/*Operations interfaces which must NOT have it).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Interfaces: the rule inverts — *Endpoints/*Operations must NOT have @friendlyName; all others must. Getting this backwards produces an error.

key_file_guides.role: Warns when 2+ fields share the same underscore prefix within a model (e.g. billing_id, billing_status → should be billing.id, billing.status). EXCLUDED_PREFIXES list exempts AIP-standard prefixes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New prefixes that should be exempt must be added to EXCLUDED_PREFIXES or they will trigger warnings on all existing uses.

key_file_guides.role: Warns against `field: T | null` — use `field?: T` instead for optional properties.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only fires on non-optional (`!property.node?.optional`) union properties that include a null variant.

key_file_guides.role: Error rule enforcing kebab-case on explicit `@operationId` decorator values.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only checks properties with explicit @operationId — auto-generated operation IDs are not checked.

key_file_guides.role: Warning rule discouraging `extends` without `@discriminator` on base. Distinguishes template instances (different message) from plain model extension.
key_file_guides.watch_for: isTemplateInstance check — template base models get a different diagnostic message than plain model bases.

anti_patterns: Defining rules outside `createRule` (e.g. raw functions that call context.report) — TypeSpec rule registration requires createRule
Using `| null` on model properties instead of the optional `?` modifier
Adding underscore-prefixed field groups without checking EXCLUDED_PREFIXES in field-prefix.js
Using `extends` for composition without adding `@discriminator` to the base model
Omitting `@friendlyName`, `@doc`, or `@summary` on new types/operations — these are linting errors/warnings that block CI

decisions.decision: Two-severity model: errors block compilation, warnings surface in lint but don't fail build
decisions.rationale: Structural invariants (enum values must be snake_case for JSON wire format, operationId must be kebab-case for SDK method names) are errors; stylistic consistency (docs, composition preference) are warnings to allow incremental adoption.

decisions.decision: PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) codified in utils.js
decisions.rationale: AIP naming conventions acknowledge common industry acronyms; the regex would incorrectly flag OAuthToken or APIKey without the exceptions list.

decisions.decision: field-prefix rule EXCLUDED_PREFIXES exempts AIP-standard boolean-flag prefixes (is_, enable_, disable_, etc.)
decisions.rationale: AIP itself defines standard boolean field prefixes that intentionally repeat; grouping them under a nested object would violate AIP guidance.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new custom lint rule that warns when an operation lacks a @tag decorator
